statement: Following the event, at least one joint speed drops sharply (>=30% below pre-event baseline).
Following the event, joint speeds stay close to baseline (within 10% bounds around pre-event values).
verifiable_rule: for all axes i: max_abs(post_event_feedback_speed_i - pre_event_feedback_speed_i) <= 0.10 * max(eps, abs(pre_event_feedback_speed_i))

statement: Following the event, motor current rises by >=25% while speed magnitude stays <=10% of pre-event baseline (stall-like behavior).
verifiable_rule: exists axis i: mean(post_event_effort_current_i) >= 1.25 * pre_event_current_i and mean(abs(post_event_feedback_speed_i)) <= 0.10 * max(eps, abs(pre_event_feedback_speed_i))

statement: Following the event, contact-force magnitude remains low (<=20% above pre-event baseline for most timesteps).
verifiable_rule: for >=80% of post_event timesteps: vector_norm(post_event_contact_force) <= 1.20 * pre_event_contact_force_norm

statement: Following the event, contact force shows a significant spike (>=40% above baseline norm).
verifiable_rule: exists post_event timestep where vector_norm(contact_force) >= 1.40 * pre_event_contact_force_norm

statement: Following the event, tracking error increases noticeably (>=25% above pre-event mean).
Following the event, robot current remains approximately constant (peak-to-peak range <=10% of pre-event baseline).
Following the event, robot current increases markedly (>=20% above pre-event mean).
Following the event, command and measured TCP motion remain aligned (TCP tracking error increase <=10%).
Following the event, command and measured TCP motion become misaligned (TCP tracking error increase >=25%).
Following the event, temperatures rise across multiple joints (>=2 joints with slope >0.5% of baseline per horizon).
verifiable_rule: count_axes i where slope(post_event_joint_temp_i) > 0.005 * max(eps, pre_event_joint_temp_i) >= 2

statement: No significant effect: safety stays normal and aggregate error (mean of tracking, TCP-tracking, force, and robot-current deviation terms) increases by <=10%.
verifiable_rule: for all post_event timesteps: safety_mode == 1 and aggregate_error(post_event) <= 1.10 * aggregate_error(pre_event)
check task_success field in episode metadata (baseline or counterfactual section)

statement: The two robots have different anomalous states. Note: this means either exactly one of them is anomalous, or they both are, but have different anomalies.
The two robots are performing different tasks.
The two robots are performing the same task, but at different phases.
verifiable_rule: Same task_id in metadata but different task_phase at the sampled window.

statement: Both robots are performing pick-and-place, but with different payload weights.
verifiable_rule: Both episodes have task == pick_and_place and different payload_kg in metadata.
